reviews.review_msg: Mike is probably the best professor I've had so far at SFU. His explanations in lectures are so clear and he actually goes through examples instead of just reading off of slides. Initially, I was concerned about taking this course as I did not do the best in BUS 232, but I was able to achieve a good mark thanks to his teaching.
He is not a good guy to take with. Make the course more harder as a very difficult level. Not recommend

I am thankful that he teaches with Excel instead of R. That said, attend every lecture; exams include lecture-specific content like the Indigo case and Tableau. Master the how, not just the what because he tests keystrokes on calculator and Excel formulas. He is a very nice guy. Don't be afraid to ask him questions on your group project or concept.
His teaching is okay, and he is a really nice knowledgeble person. But he made his class so hard to study with, the contents and the exam difficulties with length could not manageble..
OMG so many contents and exams really A LOT! His exams are not clear to let students know what is asking to understand and he let this course = graduate studies level. Would not recommend..
Teaching is so hard and quite confused to let the undergraduate students to learn, he usually be teaching MBA class, so the difficulties are really challenged!
Pretty hard course and harsh instructor, tests are EXTREMELLY HARD!

Would not recommend. There is more group work than actual learning. I feel like I barely learned the actual content as I was more stressed about the group work than the actual content. His wording on the exam is hard to understand

He wouldn't fix the link to his class/sessions and proceeded to just continue when many student's couldn't get in. We pay for this teacher to teach us but he doesn't even care if half the class can't even get into his zoom link
Michael is the best instructor for this course; this point is definitely without any doubts.
He is just amazing!!! The Best Professor I ever had at SFU :)

I really enjoy taking 336 with Michael! He cares about his students and keeps everything organized. The course is not easy but he makes it easier by giving some examples in class. Make sure to review the textbook and tutorial slides prior to the lecture and do weekly practice questions to keep track of the material.

Michael was a great prof who tried to explain the concepts clearly. However, the marking was left to the TAs and he wasn't very proactive in answering emails. Midterm and final were a bit confusing as everything is now online. Study and pay attention to his lectures
This guy loves coffee, and you should too! He will mention coffee 20+ times in each of his lecture recordings and for you to drink some. Coffee break! literally great teaching skills
One of the best profs I've ever had.
